title: Expect 100-continue, Early Responses, Body Rejection, and Draining · Execution Trace: Expect Deadlock Proof
summary: A server reads a huge body before authentication, sends 100 after a final response, drains attacker bytes without bounds, and lets client and server wait on each other forever. Planned artifact: Add and verify the expect deadlock proof increment in expectation and early-response coordination state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test client and server state transitions under delayed, missing, duplicate, fragmented, and rejected expectations without deadlock.
Choose bounded drain versus connection close after early rejection based on remaining framing, risk, reuse value, and deadline.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Expect Deadlock Proof · Theory Expect Deadlock Proof
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1220 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to coordinate 100-continue and bounded early rejection without deadlock or unsafe reuse; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test client and server state transitions under delayed, missing, duplicate, fragmented, and rejected expectations without deadlock.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Expect Deadlock Proof contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Build Expect Deadlock Proof evidence · Theory Expect Deadlock Proof

steps.title: Defend the Expect Deadlock Proof model · Theory Expect Deadlock Proof
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Expect Deadlock Proof: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory protocol team handles case 1226 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to coordinate 100-continue and bounded early rejection without deadlock or unsafe reuse; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test client and server state transitions under delayed, missing, duplicate, fragmented, and rejected expectations without deadlock.
